methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Amperon is a Series B-stage infrastructure and logistics company founded by Sean Kelly that has raised $20M to date. The comparable universe in this sector is sparse: only one private peer (AiDash) was identified, with no public comparables available. This limited peer set constrains the valuation methodology and introduces material uncertainty around cross-company benchmarking.

Stage-progression analysis is the primary applicable method given the peer scarcity. Using total raised ($20M) as a proxy base value and applying a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x yields an implied valuation of $38M. The peer-median approach was deemed inapplicable: while one same-sector peer exists, the narrow filter requirements (same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier) did not yield the minimum three datapoints needed for reliable cross-company comparison. Relying on an all-sector median would risk conflating business models and risk profiles across disparate infrastructure subsegments.

The triangulated analysis, anchored to the single stage-progression method, supports a valuation range of $32M (low) to $44M (high), with a median of $38M. The range reflects reasonable bounds around the step-up calculation rather than independent method convergence. Key uncertainties include the absence of sector-specific peer data, the reliance on stage-progression mechanics alone, and unknown unit economics or growth metrics for Amperon. A fuller valuation would require either expanded comparable data or disclosure of revenue, customer concentration, and cash-burn metrics.
